intitule: Reading Comprehension: Identifying Perpetrators and Reasons

enonce.texte:
Read the text below carefully and answer the questions that follow.

A Harmful Tradition

In many parts of the world, thousands of girls are subjected to female genital mutilation (FGM) every year. This practice, which is often justified by cultural or religious beliefs, involves the partial or total removal of the external female genitalia. The perpetrators of this act are frequently family members, such as mothers or grandmothers, who believe they are acting in the best interest of the girl. They think it will preserve her honor, ensure her marriageability, or control her sexuality. However, FGM is a gross violation of human rights. It causes severe pain, long-term health problems, and psychological trauma for the victims, who are often too young to consent. Several international organizations, including the WHO, have condemned this practice and are working with local communities to end it.
images: A simple, respectful illustration showing a group of women and girls in a rural African village setting, with a community leader or health worker talking to them. No graphic content.

questions.texte: Identify the main social issue discussed in the text.
questions.reponse: The main social issue discussed is female genital mutilation (FGM).

questions.texte: List the perpetrators of this act as mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: The perpetrators are frequently family members, such as mothers or grandmothers.

questions.texte: Explain the reasons why family members justify the practice of FGM.
questions.reponse: They justify it because they believe it will preserve the girl's honor, ensure her marriageability, or control her sexuality. These are seen as cultural or religious requirements.

questions.texte: Analyze the consequences of FGM on the victims as described in the text.
questions.reponse: The consequences include severe pain, long-term health problems, and psychological trauma.

questions.texte: Evaluate the role of international organizations like the WHO in response to this social ill.
questions.reponse: International organizations have condemned the practice and are working with local communities to end it, showing a proactive and collaborative approach to eradication.

enonce.texte: Match the vocabulary words from the text with their correct definitions.

questions.texte: Match the following words with their definitions.

sousQuestions.reponse: 3. A person who carries out a harmful, illegal, or immoral act.
4. The act of severely damaging or cutting off a part of the body.
1. A deeply distressing or disturbing experience.
2. Permission for something to happen or agreement to do something.

questions.texte: Use the word 'perpetrator' in a new sentence of your own.
questions.reponse: The police are still searching for the perpetrators of the violent crime. (Accept any grammatically correct sentence that demonstrates understanding of the word.)

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences below by choosing the correct relative pronoun (who, which, or that). In some cases, more than one answer may be possible.

questions.texte: A perpetrator is a person ______ commits a harmful act.
questions.reponse: A perpetrator is a person who/that commits a harmful act.

questions.texte: FGM is a practice ______ is often justified by cultural beliefs.
questions.reponse: FGM is a practice which/that is often justified by cultural beliefs.

questions.texte: The victims, ______ are usually young girls, suffer from trauma.
questions.reponse: The victims, who are usually young girls, suffer from trauma. (Note: 'that' is not typically used in a non-defining clause like this, so 'who' is the best answer).

questions.texte: The organization ______ works to end FGM is the WHO.
questions.reponse: The organization which/that works to end FGM is the WHO.

questions.texte: The campaign, ______ was launched last year, has been very effective.
questions.reponse: The campaign, which was launched last year, has been very effective. (Note: 'that' is not typically used in a non-defining clause like this, so 'which' is the best answer).

enonce.texte:
You are a youth leader in your community. You have learned about the dangers of a social ill like female genital mutilation (FGM) or child marriage. Your task is to create a short awareness campaign for your village. You will write a one-paragraph message (150-200 words) to be read at a community gathering. In your message, you must:

  • Define the social ill (e.g., using a relative pronoun).
  • Identify the perpetrators and explain their motivations.
  • Describe the consequences for the victims.
  • Propose a solution to end the practice.
images: A photo of a young adult speaking to a group of elders and villagers in a community meeting setting under a tree.

questions.texte: Write your awareness campaign message below. Remember to structure your argument clearly and address the community with respect.
questions.reponse:
**Exemple de réponse (non-exhaustif):**

My fellow villagers, today I wish to speak about a practice that has been a part of our culture for generations, but which is deeply harmful. This practice, which is called female genital mutilation or FGM, is a violation of our girls' rights. The perpetrators, who are often our own mothers and grandmothers, believe they are preserving the girls' honor. However, this act, which causes severe pain, can lead to life-long health problems and psychological trauma for the victims, who are too young to consent. We are not asking you to reject our culture, but to protect our children. Let's work together to create a future where every girl is safe and healthy. We can do this by educating our community and supporting alternative rites of passage that celebrate our girls without harming them.
